mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and impending economic contraction, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0% over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with artificially low policy rates compress real yields and trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, yielding a positive Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum shows zero predictive power for 5-day returns when GDP growth exceeds 4% annually, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Higher retail sales volumes reflect robust consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, directly translating to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth consistently fails to correlate with equity returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 300,000 jobs signals strong labor demand and rising household income, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll surges above 300,000 are followed by negative 60-day equity returns in more than 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates a synchronized economic recovery, boosting corporate profit expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when industrial production growth decouples from labor market improvements, resulting in a negative Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Surging housing starts coupled with accelerating inflation signal overheating demand and impending monetary tightening, causing investors to price in higher discount rates and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts and inflation jointly fail to predict negative returns when the yield curve remains steep, producing a positive Rank IC instead.
